title: The Blue And The Gray

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh! catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated character moment with maximum economy

jokes.text: I love our Valentine's Day tradition of going out with each other's sisters. Is there anything better than my best friend's face on a girl body?
jokes.setup: Valentine's Day tradition between friends
jokes.punchline: The disturbing mental image of wanting your friend's face on a woman's body
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise that gets funnier the more you think about it, good character moment for these supporting characters

jokes.text: Stir a drop of Jagermeister into some pink lemonade, slice in some strawberry ChapStick, call it Cupid's ambrosia
jokes.setup: Moe creating a Valentine's Day cocktail
jokes.punchline: The disgusting ingredient combination including ChapStick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - sleazy bartender making gross drinks with fancy names

jokes.text: Do you have plans for tonight? What, are you crazy? Of course I do. Got a hot date. Come on, scram, so I can get ready.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming to have Valentine's plans
jokes.punchline: The transparent lie that he's trying to maintain dignity
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe defense mechanism - lying about having social plans, very character-specific

jokes.text: I'm telling you, I'm fine. I never been happier. See? There. There, that's a smile, right? Showing teeth, eyes all crinkly.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to convince himself he's happy alone
jokes.punchline: The desperate over-analysis of his own forced smile
jokes.explanation: Pathetic and relatable moment of self-deception, good cringe comedy

jokes.text: Even you let me down, Hitler!
jokes.setup: Moe watching a Hitler documentary on Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Being personally disappointed by Hitler of all people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the absurdity of having expectations of Hitler that could be let down

jokes.text: your love life is like Sister Act 3-- no Whoopi!
jokes.setup: TV host addressing lonely viewers
jokes.punchline: Comparing their love life to a non-existent sequel without the star
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference with wordplay, though not character-specific

jokes.text: I knew my love life was like that movie somehow.
jokes.setup: Following the Sister Act 3 comparison
jokes.punchline: Moe's resigned acceptance of the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe response - self-aware and defeated, great character consistency

jokes.text: turn from couch potato to sex tornado
jokes.setup: Dating seminar advertisement
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous alliteration and imagery
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but fairly standard infomercial parody territory

jokes.text: Drive my kids to school! Never!
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to drive the kids
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic refusal as if it's an outrageous request
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer lazy parenting moment with appropriate dramatic delivery

jokes.text: Stupid double snuggler's hitch! Only hope is to chew off my leg! Ow! Mmm, not bad. Just 127 more hours.
jokes.setup: Homer tangled in bedsheets
jokes.punchline: 127 Hours movie reference with Homer actually tasting his leg
jokes.explanation: Great movie parody with perfect Homer twist - of course he'd taste his own leg

jokes.text: Homer's reaction to Marge finding gray hair - awkward silence and topic change
jokes.setup: Marge discovers her first gray hair
jokes.punchline: Homer's visible discomfort and inability to respond
jokes.explanation: Standard marital comedy beat but well-executed for character

jokes.text: A lot of movie stars have gray hair, like all those women we loved in the '80s.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Marge about gray hair
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment about '80s actresses being old
jokes.explanation: Homer's well-meaning but terrible attempt at reassurance, good character moment

jokes.text: Homie, you always mean to say the nicest things. Well, it's not easy with you talking all the time.
jokes.setup: Marge giving Homer credit for trying to be nice
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately proving he can't help himself from being rude
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with immediate undermining of Marge's kindness, classic Homer

jokes.text: is this the seminar where you learn how to pick up free escorts? Well, we're not learning how to fold cloth napkins.
jokes.setup: Barney asking about the dating seminar
jokes.punchline: Moe's non-denial that sidesteps the question
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Moe's typical sleazy evasiveness

jokes.text: Once we were single-celled organisms who could replicate whenever we wanted. We were men back then!
jokes.setup: Self-help guru talking about evolutionary history
jokes.punchline: Romanticizing asexual reproduction as the height of masculinity
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist take on masculinity and evolution, good unexpected angle

jokes.text: It's hell to meet girls when you're paid in chickens.
jokes.setup: Willie explaining his dating problems
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about being paid in chickens
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character detail - specific and absurd enough to be memorable

jokes.text: all I do is get drunk and kill squirrels with me shovel.
jokes.setup: Willie describing himself to women
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of squirrel murder
jokes.explanation: Perfectly horrible Willie detail delivered with his accent and casual attitude

jokes.text: Hi. I'm William. I'm a connoisseur of fine spirits, and I love animals to death.
jokes.setup: Dr. Kissingher translating Willie's crude description
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic translation that's technically accurate but misleading
jokes.explanation: Brilliant translation joke where every word is technically true but completely recontextualized

jokes.text: Ah! A monster! Save me! Sorry, that was unprofessional. Please, go on.
jokes.setup: Moe approaching for help
jokes.punchline: The professional's visceral reaction to Moe's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Moe ugliness joke with professional recovery, well-structured

jokes.text: As a kid, I had roundworm. Heck, I was more worm than boy for a couple of years.
jokes.setup: Moe sharing personal information
jokes.punchline: The disturbing detail about being more worm than boy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moe character moment - disgusting, specific, and delivered with his typical matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: I dabbled in Satanism until I was asked to leave.
jokes.setup: Moe continuing his dating profile
jokes.punchline: Being kicked out of Satanism implies he was too much even for them
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating joke - if you're too extreme for Satanists, that's saying something

jokes.text: He can't more handsome or appealing than you. Let's take a flip through the old Moe-lodex.
jokes.setup: Explaining wingman requirements and Moe looking for candidates
jokes.punchline: The pun on 'Rolodex' and the implication that Moe's contacts are limited and sad
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that works with Moe's character, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: What if I see her hand at the same time you see her face? Face beats hand!
jokes.setup: Discussing wingman rules about who saw a woman first
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of body part hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic joke about dating rules taken to ridiculous extremes

jokes.text: Homer had the basketball game muted the whole time.
jokes.setup: Marge describing her romantic Valentine's dinner
jokes.punchline: The pathetically low bar for Homer's romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's low expectations for Homer's attention

jokes.text: You've been grayer than a Seattle Cinco de Mayo for years now.
jokes.setup: Revealing Marge has been dyeing her hair
jokes.punchline: The specific cultural reference joke about Seattle's lack of Hispanic celebration
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural observation with good specificity, though not character-specific

jokes.text: It's a battle of the Blue and Gray here, and I am Gaybraham Lincoln, baby.
jokes.setup: Civil War hair color metaphor
jokes.punchline: The gay Abraham Lincoln pun
jokes.explanation: Good pun that ties to the episode title and character, economical delivery

jokes.text: Homer's internal debate about how to respond to Marge's hair
jokes.setup: Marge asking what Homer thinks of her new hair
jokes.punchline: Homer's visible internal panic about giving the right answer
jokes.explanation: Classic marital comedy beat, well-executed for Homer's character

jokes.text: We could fake a stroke. The last time we faked a stroke, it led to a real one.
jokes.setup: Homer's brain suggesting escape strategies
jokes.punchline: The darkly comic escalation about fake medical emergencies
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy with perfect Homer logic - of course his fake stroke became real

jokes.text: So... chicken.
jokes.setup: Desperate need for a response about Marge's hair
jokes.punchline: Homer deflecting to the completely unrelated chicken he brought
jokes.explanation: Perfect coward's deflection with maximum economy, classic Homer avoidance

jokes.text: I know I use the word 'empowering' a lot, but this time, it really is that.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on Marge's hair choice
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness about overusing buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment showing self-awareness about her liberal vocabulary

jokes.text: I just have one question about hair. Where does mine start? Head, head, head, hair. Where's the border?
jokes.setup: Discussion about hair
jokes.punchline: Bart's existential crisis about the hairline boundary
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor that kids would actually think about

jokes.text: What are we?
jokes.setup: Following Bart's hairline question
jokes.punchline: Baby Maggie having an existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with baby Maggie joining the existential crisis, maximum economy

jokes.text: you've always been beautiful, but now you're my silver belle.
jokes.setup: Homer finally responding to Marge's hair
jokes.punchline: The sweet silver belle pun
jokes.explanation: Sweet pun that works for Homer's character, though not particularly original

jokes.text: I feel like I'm married to Richard Gere.
jokes.setup: Homer's private thoughts about Marge's gray hair
jokes.punchline: The specific Richard Gere comparison
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Homer's perspective on attractiveness

jokes.text: I'm just going to draw a hairline on.
jokes.setup: Homer stressing about his own hair issues
jokes.punchline: His simple, absurd solution to baldness
jokes.explanation: Simple Homer solution that's visually funny but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Like West Virginia does for Virginia.
jokes.setup: Explaining wingman concept of making someone look good by comparison
jokes.punchline: The state comparison joke
jokes.explanation: Smart geographical humor with good economy, fits Frink's intellectual character

jokes.text: You ever notice that pizzas have gotten so small lately? They're like dimes.
jokes.setup: Lenny engaging the less attractive friend
jokes.punchline: The specific pizza size comparison
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor, gets the job done but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: No, they just sawed off the ends and painted over the nubs.
jokes.setup: Moe asking about Lenny's rebar injury
jokes.punchline: The casual description of horrific medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy about Springfield's terrible healthcare, perfect for Lenny

jokes.text: Oh! Judi Dench?! Kiss me, 007.
jokes.setup: Being told to imagine woman as Bond girl
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinking of the oldest possible Bond actress
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke that subverts expectations perfectly with economy

jokes.text: They should warn you when he's coming.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Count von Count from Sesame Street
jokes.punchline: Treating him like a horror movie villain
jokes.explanation: Good kid's perspective on children's TV that adults can relate to

jokes.text: Normally, I think your mom is hot... Take that back! I'm sorry. What I mean is, she's not hot anymore. Take that back!
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying to comment on Marge's appearance
jokes.punchline: Making it worse with every attempt to clarify
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - awkward kid making everything worse, good escalation

jokes.text: Just like my mom on her 'can't dance' days.
jokes.setup: Comparing Bart's moodiness
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific family detail about mom's dancing restrictions
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and weird family detail that fits Milhouse perfectly

jokes.text: I hope I look half as good as you when I give up.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Marge's gray hair choice
jokes.punchline: Framing it as 'giving up' rather than being natural
jokes.explanation: Good passive-aggressive insult disguised as a compliment

jokes.text: Grandma had hair like that when she went to sleep in her forever box.
jokes.setup: Child commenting on Marge's hair
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to death and corpse appearance
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor from child's innocent perspective with memorable phrasing

jokes.text: They'll give four diamonds to any place that's got a lid on the toilet.
jokes.setup: Complaining about AAA restaurant ratings
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously low standards for high ratings
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about grade inflation - toilet lid as luxury amenity

jokes.text: Well, you know, maybe you belong to the A.A... The A.A.? No! Oh, no, I meant the A.A... A.A.A.? No, I'm not in Triple-A. I don't like their Westways magazine. They'll give four diamonds to any place that's got a lid on the toilet. No, I meant the A.A... R... P?! The A.A.R.P.?!
jokes.setup: Cashier trying to ask about senior discount
jokes.punchline: The escalating misunderstanding and Marge's horror at being asked about AARP
jokes.explanation: Good escalating misunderstanding with realistic cringe about aging assumptions

jokes.text: I can imagine the pepperonis are Swiss chalets nestled in a big layer of mozzarella snow!
jokes.setup: Old employee's excitement about putting back groceries
jokes.punchline: The elaborate fantasy about frozen pizza case
jokes.explanation: Sweet and absurd visualization from elderly character finding joy in simple things

jokes.text: That's an excessive number of times.
jokes.setup: Description of Bart punching Milhouse '50 billion times'
jokes.punchline: Skinner's dry, administrative response to the hyperbole
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner deadpan response with excellent economy and character consistency

jokes.text: Thank God. I don't want to, either.
jokes.setup: Bart saying he doesn't want to talk about his problems
jokes.punchline: Skinner's relieved agreement, abandoning his counselor role
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Skinner's discomfort with counseling duties

jokes.text: They recently learned they were once two-thirds of conjoined triplets. And the third one is out for revenge.
jokes.setup: Explaining why not to judge the counselors
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous backstory about revenge-seeking triplet
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd throwaway explanation with horror movie elements

jokes.text: They recently learned they were once two-thirds of conjoined triplets. And the third one is out for revenge.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining why not to judge Sherri and Terri harshly
jokes.punchline: The completely absurd backstory involving separated triplets and revenge
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd throwaway backstory that's both dark and unexpected, highly original

jokes.text: Your hair committed blue-icide.
jokes.setup: Patty commenting on Marge's hair change
jokes.punchline: The suicide/blue-icide pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Patty's antagonistic relationship with Marge

jokes.text: That means he hates it. If he really liked it, he'd just paw at it instead of getting all clever.
jokes.setup: Homer's 'silver belle' comment
jokes.punchline: The insight that Homer's cleverness indicates discomfort
jokes.explanation: Excellent insight into Homer's character - when he's trying to be clever, he's uncomfortable

jokes.text: Ma'am, are you sure that's wise, with what I presume is your heart condition?
jokes.setup: Marge ordering a double espresso
jokes.punchline: Barista assuming she has health problems due to gray hair
jokes.explanation: Serviceable ageism joke but relies on stereotypes, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Look out, chicks. The silver fox is coming to the henhouse.
jokes.setup: Marge psyching herself up for the club
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at sexy confidence using predator/prey metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge trying to be confident in an uncharacteristic way

jokes.text: What, did he eat your candy house and push you into the oven?
jokes.setup: Marge looking for Homer at the bar
jokes.punchline: The witch from Hansel and Gretel comparison
jokes.explanation: Solid fairy tale comparison for Marge's appearance, though not particularly original

jokes.text: I've never seen a drunk hold his vomit like you. I do keep it together.
jokes.setup: Woman complimenting Homer's drinking ability
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud acceptance of this disgusting compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment - taking pride in something disgusting

jokes.text: I've never seen a drunk hold his vomit like you. I do keep it together.
jokes.setup: Woman complimenting Homer's drinking ability
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud acceptance of this disgusting compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Homer taking pride in his drinking 'skills'

jokes.text: I flew the first packet of royal mail across the Khyber Pass, dodging the poison-tipped spears of the dreaded Zim-Zam Tribe.
jokes.setup: Burns as wingman telling stories
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and probably false adventure story
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns tall tale with perfect period detail and character voice

jokes.text: Which of you wants to be the sliced haddock in a geezer sandwich?
jokes.setup: Burns addressing potential romantic partners
jokes.punchline: His characteristically bizarre food metaphor for a threesome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line combining his old-fashioned language with inappropriate sexual content

jokes.text: This is a situation I call 'The Doctor Is O.'.'
jokes.setup: Complicated romantic situation at the bar
jokes.punchline: The play on 'The Doctor Is In' with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but relies on cheap sexual humor, fits the character though

jokes.text: Including Val Kilmer from Top Gun, Wedge Antilles from Star Wars, and me, from now.
jokes.setup: Homer listing famous wingmen
jokes.punchline: Adding himself to the list with 'from now' as his credential
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer characteristically placing himself among fictional heroes

jokes.text: Well, as a feminist, virtually anything a woman does is empowering. Oh. Is my job creating power, empowering? No. It's oddly dehumanizing.
jokes.setup: Lisa's feminist perspective on empowerment
jokes.punchline: The contradiction when asked about Homer's actual job
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of empty empowerment rhetoric with perfect Lisa character work

jokes.text: Oh, hello, young man! Where's your father? He's dying in a retirement home. Now kiss me.
jokes.setup: Old woman greeting Bart
jokes.punchline: Her casual mention of death followed by inappropriate advance
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with unexpected escalation, efficient delivery of disturbing content

tmdb_overview: Moe attends a seminar in hope of gaining more confidence with women; Marge makes a change after discovering her gray hair.
